_source: OmniClass Table 13 — 13-45 Library Spaces (secondary mirror declared in sprint-X-source-provenance.md §1.2 and _source/OmniClass-Table-13-source-notes.md)
_note: Per-entry _verification_status records sourcing. cross_references.* populated at X.D.1 back-fill (currently all null). The 13-45 00 00 umbrella entry is excluded from entries[] as the parent_category header. The secondary mirror (pdfcoffee) exposes one unambiguous sub-entry beneath the umbrella: 13-45 11 00 Library. A second code 13-45 11 11 appears in the mirror but carries a definition belonging to spiritual spaces (probable OCR/layout artefact in the pdfcoffee scan); that entry is therefore NOT treated as mirror_sourced. The Scribd corroboration mirror returned no 13-45 content. Consequently 1 entry is mirror_sourced and 9 entries are inferred from (a) the OmniClass hierarchical numbering pattern observed across peer categories (13-41 Museum Spaces, 13-37 Artistic Spaces), and (b) the canonical library MEP space taxonomy documented in ASHRAE Handbook — HVAC Applications Chapter 23 (Museums, Libraries, and Archives), CIBSE LG5:2015 (The SLL Lighting Guide: Lecture, Study and Educational Buildings), BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.36 (library illuminance recommendations), and SCONUL (Society of College, National and University Libraries) space planning guidance. Each inferred entry carries a mandatory _inference_note citing the relevant authority. The ifc_space_type follows IfcSpaceTypeEnum (ISO 16739): all library spaces in this sub-tree are enclosed interior spaces (INTERNAL). Future Sprint Y back-fill from the canonical OCCS PDF (CSI license) will raise _verification_status from mirror_sourced/inferred to occs_verified and may add or refine codes.

entries._inference_note: Level-4 code 13-45 11 11 synthesised from OmniClass hierarchical numbering pattern (peer: 13-41 11 11 permanent collection gallery). Library stacks are the primary sub-type within a library building, distinguished from reading rooms in ASHRAE Chapter 23 (Museums, Libraries, and Archives) — stacks have distinct HVAC requirements (lower occupancy density, tighter RH control for collection preservation per BS EN 15757:2010 §5). Code not confirmed in pdfcoffee or Scribd mirrors for 13-45.

Level-4 code 13-45 11 13 synthesised from OmniClass numbering pattern (odd-numbered leaves within a group: 11, 13, 15 …). Reading rooms are the principal patron-occupied space in any library; they appear as a distinct space type in BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.36 (500 lux maintained illuminance on the reading plane) and ASHRAE Chapter 23 which treats reading rooms and stacks as separate HVAC zones. Code not confirmed in pdfcoffee or Scribd mirrors.

Level-4 code 13-45 11 15 synthesised from OmniClass leaf-numbering pattern (13, 15, 17 within group 11). Study carrels are the individual enclosed or semi-enclosed study units standard in academic and research libraries; they appear as a distinct design type in SCONUL Space Planning in Academic Libraries (2011) and are referenced in CIBSE LG5:2015 as requiring supplementary task lighting above the general 500 lux reading-room level. Code not confirmed in mirror.

Level-3 group code 13-45 13 00 synthesised from OmniClass inter-group numbering (11 → 13 → 15 within 13-45). Library archives and special collections are a distinct space type with separate environmental control requirements: ASHRAE Chapter 23 specifies 15–21 °C / 30–50 % RH for rare book and manuscript storage, contrasting with general stack conditions. The British Standard BS 5454:2000 (now superseded by BS EN 16893:2018) also distinguishes archive accommodation from open-access library space. Code not confirmed in mirror.

Level-4 code 13-45 13 11 synthesised from OmniClass numbering pattern (13-41 13 11 compact mobile storage as peer). Rare books rooms require the tightest environmental control of any library space — BS EN 16893:2018 §6.3 specifies set-point stability of ±1 °C / ±5 % RH for parchment and vellum; ASHRAE Chapter 23 Table 3 lists Class A storage conditions. Treated as a distinct space type from general archive. Code not confirmed in mirror.

Level-3 group code 13-45 15 00 synthesised from OmniClass inter-group numbering (13-41 15 00 conservation laboratory as peer). Technical services — cataloguing, acquisitions, bindery intake — are recognised as a separate functional zone in SCONUL Space Planning in Academic Libraries (2011) and in the PFI/NHS library design guides (requiring staff-grade HVAC and task lighting distinct from patron reading rooms). Code not confirmed in mirror.

Level-4 code 13-45 15 11 synthesised from OmniClass leaf numbering (13-41 15 11 wet conservation lab as peer). Librarian and library-administration offices are standard accommodation in every library brief and appear as a distinct HVAC zone in ASHRAE Chapter 23 (office occupancy density and ventilation rates differ from reading rooms and stacks). Code not confirmed in mirror.

Level-3 group code 13-45 17 00 synthesised from OmniClass inter-group numbering (13-41 17 00 museum archive as peer). Media rooms (AV playback, microfilm/microfiche readers, digital listening stations) are a documented distinct space type in library design guides — SCONUL Space Planning (2011) §4.3 and CIBSE LG5:2015 both note that AV spaces require acoustic treatment and independent HVAC from open reading rooms due to equipment heat loads. Code not confirmed in mirror.

Level-4 code 13-45 17 11 synthesised from OmniClass leaf numbering (13-41 17 11 photographic archive as peer). Children's library sections are a widely recognised distinct space type in public library design — they appear in CIBSE LG5:2015 and in the DCMS/MLA Public Library Standards (2001) as requiring different furniture scale, lower counter heights, and enhanced acoustic separation from adult reading rooms. Placed under the media/activity group (13-45 17) rather than main stack/reading group (13-45 11) because children's libraries typically combine picture books, AV, and play elements in a single activity zone. Code not confirmed in mirror.
